title: Indulgent Italian Veal Ossobuco
description: A rich and comforting malty Italian dish featuring braised veal shanks, confit tomatoes, and a hint of milk chocolate. Perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner.
instructions: Season the veal shanks with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the veal shanks until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the veal shanks from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the confit tomatoes, white wine, beef broth, thyme, and bay leaves to the pot.
Return the veal shanks to the pot and bring the liquid to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven. Braise the veal shanks for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the milk chocolate until melted.
Return the pot to the stovetop and simmer the sauce over medium heat until thickened, about 10 minutes.
Serve the veal shanks with the sauce spooned over the top.
Garnish with fresh thyme, if desired.
